Title: Engineering Plant Cytochrome P450s for Enhanced Synthesis of Natural Products: Past Achievements and Future Perspectives

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves yeast viability and increases artemisinic acid yield by helping regulate electron transfer to P450s
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression in yeast; metabolite yield analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful auxiliary factor to optimize P450 electron transfer and raise heterologous natural-product yields
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the main pathway catalyst for the terminal metabolite; acts as electron-transfer support protein in engineered system

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate toward target product and may be repurposed for pathway gap-filling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxadiene/taxane intermediate at the 5alpha position in taxol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Engineering of N-terminal sequence and fusion with CPR improved taxol precursor production; proposed as candidate to repurpose for missing oxidation steps
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein engineering; fusion to CPR; heterologous production assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T5aH is a known taxol-pathway P450 from yew. 2. N-terminal engineering and CPR linkage improved precursor production. 3. The review suggests it could be repurposed to help fill unresolved taxol oxidation steps.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article; statements summarize prior studies rather than presenting new experiments.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate toward target product and may be repurposed for pathway gap-filling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxane intermediate at the 10beta position in taxol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Highlighted as a known P450 with substrate similarity to unidentified enzymes and potential utility for completing taxol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: prior pathway characterization cited in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T10bH is a characterized taxol-pathway P450. 2. It acts on substrates structurally similar to those of unknown taxol-pathway P450s. 3. It is proposed as a repurposing candidate for pathway completion.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No new experimental validation in this review.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: decorates a pathway intermediate to enable safe progression toward end product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Decorates 8-hydroxy-7-keto-d-cadinene and prevents phytotoxicity during gossypol synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pathway characterization and metabolite/intermediate analysis in cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71BE79 is a highly active cotton P450 in gossypol biosynthesis. 2. It modifies 8-hydroxy-7-keto-d-cadinene. 3. This decoration step prevents phytotoxicity during pathway operation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Gossypol has anti-nutritional toxicity despite bioactivity; pathway engineering may need to redirect flux to less toxic intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes that gossypol pathway genes are dispersed and its P450s belong to different families.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates geraniol at C8 en route to strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Codon optimization and additional gene copies increased G8H expression and raised strictosidine production titer in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: codon optimization; gene copy-number increase; heterologous metabolite production assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G8H is a plant P450 used in strictosidine pathway reconstruction. 2. Expression optimization improved enzyme-host compatibility. 3. Higher G8H expression increased strictosidine titer in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key engineering target for improving monoterpene indole alkaloid production in heterologous hosts.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: improves flux toward desired product by tuning substrate preference
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Substrate-recognition engineering achieved a 5.6-fold activity increase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein engineering of substrate recognition sites; activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH15 activity was limited by substrate specificity. 2. Tuning substrate preference improved catalytic performance. 3. Engineered variants showed 5.6-fold higher activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates usefulness of substrate-recognition engineering for boosting plant P450 performance.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fusion to bacterial P450 reductase domain generated the first active P450 fusion enzyme and improved functional electron transfer
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: bacterial P450 reductase domain from Rhodococcus sp.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Soybean isoflavone synthase 1 was fused to a reductase domain. 2. The fusion produced an active artificial P450 enzyme. 3. Improved proximity to redox partner enhanced functionality.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful design model for constructing self-sufficient P450s in metabolic engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: With low CPR and normal CYP71AV1 expression, addition of cytochrome b5 improved yeast viability and artemisinic acid yield
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway engineering; co-expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71AV1 is a key P450 for artemisinic acid formation in yeast. 2. Electron transfer balance strongly affects productivity. 3. Cytochrome b5 co-expression improved yield and host fitness.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Imbalanced electron transfer can cause uncoupling and reactive oxygen species toxicity.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux toward blue pigment branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression together with cytochrome b5 is required for accumulation of blue pigment in carnations
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP75A promotes blue pigment formation. 2. Efficient activity requires cytochrome b5 support. 3. Co-overexpression is needed for blue coloration in carnation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A practical target for engineering blue flower color.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation is associated with pink flower color in cyclamen
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP75B influences anthocyanin branch choice. 2. Reduced CYP75B expression shifts flower pigmentation. 3. Downregulation yields pink color in cyclamen.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful branch-control target for ornamental color engineering.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: redirects metabolic flux from cucurbitacin C toward mogrol in a de novo pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Engineered from a multifunctional P450 into a monooxygenase to create a de novo pathway channeling flux from cucurbitacin C toward mogrol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: computational design with Rosetta and co-evolving amino acid information; pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP87D20 was redesigned using computational and evolutionary information. 2. Engineering converted it from multifunctional activity to monooxygenase behavior. 3. The redesign supported a de novo pathway from cucurbitacin C to mogrol.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates how computation-guided P450 redesign can generate new high-value triterpenoid pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as proof-of-concept summarized from prior study.
